title: Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Kerala

overview.short: Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located along the Western Ghats in Chalakudy taluk of Thrissur District of Kerala state in India.
overview.description: Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located along the Western Ghats in Chalakudy taluk of Thrissur District of Kerala state in India. Established in 1984, the sanctuary with an area of about 85.067 km2 is on the western slopes of the Nelliyampathi Hills. The highest peak in sanctuary is Punda Peak (1116 m). Along with the neighboring Peechi-Vazhani Wildlife Sanctuary it forms a continuous protected area of 210 km2.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chimmini Dam
topPlaces.description: Chimmini Dam is situated in Echippara in Chalakudy taluk of Thrissur District of Kerala state of India. It is constructed across Kurumali river, a tributary of the Karuvannur river.

topPlaces.name: Peechi-Vazhani Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Peechi-Vazhani Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary headquartered in Peechi, Thrissur District of Kerala, India. The sanctuary was established in 1958 consisting of Palappilli- Nelliyampathi forests including the area of Chimmony Wildlife sanctuary and is the second-oldest sanctuary in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Akathiyoor
topPlaces.description: Akathiyoor is a census town in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Palakkuzhi
topPlaces.description: Palakkuzhi is a village in Alathur taluk of Palakkad district, Kerala State. Vadakkenchery is the nearest town and is 14 km away from here.

topPlaces.name: Vellikulangara
topPlaces.description: Vellikulangara is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India.

The sanctuary was established in 1958 consisting of Palappilli- Nelliyampathi forests including the area of Chimmony Wildlife sanctuary and is the second-oldest sanctuary in Kerala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (33 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chimmini Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chimmini Dam, Peechi-Vazhani Wildlife Sanctuary, Akathiyoor, Palakkuzhi, Vellikulangara.
